frameworks.name: functional subpopulation clustering
frameworks.one_line: lOFC neurons partitioned into clusters by trial-averaged response profiles

frameworks.name: value-based decision-making
frameworks.one_line: rat task framing in which lOFC contributes to trial-by-trial learning

frameworks.name: corticostriatal projection mapping
frameworks.one_line: cluster responses qualitatively compared to identified projection neurons

core_question: Do functional subpopulations of lOFC neurons differentially encode reward history versus other task variables at the time of choice?
key_assumptions: trial-averaged PSTHs and conditional firing rates reveal functional subpopulations
lOFC subpopulations may align with anatomically defined projection targets
reward history supports lOFC-dependent trial-by-trial learning
manipulations: alignment of firing rates to different task variables
two clustering feature spaces (PSTH-based and conditional rate-based)
comparison of encoding across clusters and across task epochs
key_findings: five distinct functional clusters of lOFC neurons were identified
weak encoding of reward attributes but stronger encoding of reward history, choice, and reward receipt across clusters
only one cluster encoded reward history shortly before the choice
that cluster qualitatively resembles previously identified corticostriatal projection neurons
interpretations: a specific lOFC subpopulation may integrate previous and current trial outcomes at choice time
functional subpopulations could mediate lOFC's role in trial-by-trial learning
